info.title: Wardnet Tunneller API
info.description: Wardnet Tunneller — multi-node SNI-passthrough reverse-tunnel edge

get.description: Liveness probe. Returns 200 OK when the bridge process is running. No authentication required. Used by load balancers and uptime monitors.

Upgrade to a reverse-tunnel WebSocket. The daemon dials this with a network-scoped identity JWT and keeps the connection open; the Tunneller forwards inbound TLS connections arriving at its SNI demuxer down the tunnel, and the daemon decrypts them locally so the private key never leaves the device. 

**Frame protocol** (binary WebSocket messages): 
- `CONNECT (0x01)`: node→pi, 7 bytes `[type, conn_id:u32be, dest_port:u16be]` - `READY   (0x02)`: pi→node, 5 bytes `[type, conn_id:u32be]` - `DATA    (0x03)`: both, 5+N bytes `[type, conn_id:u32be, payload…]` - `CLOSE   (0x04)`: both, 5 bytes `[type, conn_id:u32be]` - `PING    (0x05)`: pi→node, 5 bytes (conn_id=0) - `PONG    (0x06)`: node→pi, 5 bytes (conn_id=0)

403.description: Not a network-scoped daemon, or network/subscription not eligible

ErrorBody.description: JSON body returned for every error response.
